topic: Google ADK Tutorial: Build Multi-Agent Systems with Python
keyword: Google ADK agent development kit tutorial

key_points: Official Google Developers Blog announcement of ADK at Google Cloud Next 2025
Covers ADK as a code-first, open-source framework optimized for Gemini but model-agnostic
Explains hierarchical agent trees, rich tool ecosystem, and Vertex AI deployment
Introduces A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol for cross-framework agent communication
Step-by-step Google Codelabs guide for building multi-agent systems with ADK
Covers moving from simple agent hierarchies to automated collaborative workflows
Hands-on code with sequential and parallel workflow agent patterns
Uses real Google Cloud infrastructure and Vertex AI deployment
Google Cloud Blog deep dive on multi-agent system design with ADK
Explains LlmAgent, SequentialAgent, and ParallelAgent primitives
Covers session.state as shared whiteboard for agent communication
Discusses output_key pattern to avoid race conditions in parallel agents
Community guide from Google Cloud Medium publication for beginners
Covers environment setup, installing google-adk via pip, first agent creation
Code-first approach with practical Python examples
Explains integration with LiteLLM for non-Gemini model support
Developer's guide to ADK workflow patterns: Sequential, Parallel, Loop, Custom
Explains how to compose multiple specialized workflow agents
Covers state management with session.state in multi-agent coordination
Provides code examples combining SequentialAgent + ParallelAgent for complex pipelines

stats.fact: The global AI agents market was estimated at USD 7.63 billion in 2025 and is expected to reach USD 10.91 billion in 2026
stats.source: Grand View Research AI Agents Market Report 2026

stats.fact: The AI agents market is expected to grow at a 49.6% CAGR from 2026 to 2033, reaching USD 182.97 billion by 2033
stats.source: Grand View Research AI Agents Market Report 2026

stats.fact: 99% of developers building enterprise AI applications are exploring or developing AI agents
85% of enterprises have implemented or plan to implement AI agents by end of 2026
The Gemini API has roughly 2.4 million active developers, up 118% year over year as of Q3 2025
13 million developers have built with Google's generative models as of Q3 2025
84% of respondents in the Stack Overflow 2025 Developer Survey said they are using or planning to use AI tools in development
By 2028, 75% of software developers are expected to use AI coding agents, up from less than 10% in 2023
Python ADK reached v1.0.0 stable release, signifying production-ready status for agent deployment
stats.source: Google Developers Blog: What's new with Agents - Google I/O 2025

stats.fact: ADK Python 2.0 Beta is available with workflows and agent teams; ADK TypeScript 1.0 is now available as of 2026

angles: Practical code-first tutorial approach: show readers how to go from zero to a working multi-agent system in under 30 minutes
Google ADK vs LangGraph/CrewAI: why choose ADK for Google Cloud-native stacks and Gemini-optimized workflows
Deep dive on the three agent types: LlmAgent (brain), SequentialAgent/ParallelAgent (workflow), and Custom agents (specialists)
A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol as the killer feature: how ADK agents can communicate with agents built in LangGraph, CrewAI, or other frameworks
Session state as the shared whiteboard pattern: avoiding race conditions in parallel multi-agent workflows
Real-world use cases: code review pipeline, customer support escalation, research summarization workflows
ADK's developer tooling advantage: built-in web UI for testing, tracing, and debugging agent flows locally
Production readiness: deploying ADK agents to Vertex AI Agent Engine for enterprise-grade scaling
keywords: deploy AI agent Vertex AI ADK
outline: H2: What Is Google ADK and Why It Matters in 2026
H2: Prerequisites and Installation (pip install google-adk)
H2: Core Concepts: LlmAgent, SequentialAgent, ParallelAgent, and Custom Agents
H2: Building Your First Agent: A Simple LlmAgent with Custom Tools
H2: Building a Multi-Agent Pipeline: Sequential Workflow Example
H2: Running Agents in Parallel: Speeding Up Complex Workflows
H2: Agent Communication: Session State and the Shared Whiteboard Pattern
H2: Integrating A2A Protocol for Cross-Framework Agent Communication
H2: Testing and Debugging with ADK's Built-in Dev UI
H2: Deploying Your Multi-Agent System to Vertex AI Agent Engine
H2: Google ADK vs LangGraph vs CrewAI: When to Choose Each
H2: Real-World Use Cases: Code Review, Customer Support, and Research Pipelines
